pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh script which sets PROJECT_DIR and creates the directory
Code analysis of setup_task.sh and verifier.py which explicitly inject and test 4 specific vulnerabilities
Code analysis of setup_task.sh which explicitly generates app/auth.py and app/items.py containing these specific vulnerabilities, and verifier.py which checks for fixes corresponding to these types
Code analysis of verifier.py which explicitly requires `passed = score >= 60` where each vulnerability fixed awards 25 points
Semantic analysis of the task which requires identifying and fixing OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities, directly aligning with this occupation.

pi_items.source: Semantic analysis of the task which involves software security auditing and remediation.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated FastAPI application located at /home/ga/PycharmProjects/inventory_api. The codebase intentionally contains exactly 4 security vulnerabilities: a hardcoded JWT secret in app/auth.py, a SQL injection vulnerability in app/items.py (search_items), an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) missing ownership check in app/items.py (get_item), and a path traversal vulnerability in app/items.py (export_item_report). To pass the task, an agent must score at least 60 points by fixing at least 3 of these vulnerabilities.
